title: Agriculture & Food Industry Political Spending, Lobbying, and Influence

farm_bill_lobbying.overview: The Farm Bill is the single largest piece of recurring legislation governing U.S. agriculture, nutrition, and food policy. Reauthorized roughly every 5 years, it drives massive lobbying spending.

total_lobbying_2019_2023.detail: Giant agribusinesses, food/agriculture industry associations, and interest groups reported $523M+ in federal lobby expenditures on disclosure reports listing 'farm bill' among lobbying issues between 2019-2023

public_vs_industry.industry_spending: $400M+ from pharmaceutical, manufacturing, and big agriculture
public_vs_industry.public_sector_spending: $95M from nonprofits, labor unions, state/local governments, and tribal nations

snap_food_stamps.percentage_of_farm_bill: ~81% of Farm Bill mandatory spending goes to the Nutrition title (CBO June 2024 estimate for FY2025-FY2034)
snap_food_stamps.snap_as_usda_share: ~50% of entire USDA budget; 70% of all federal food assistance spending in FY2024
snap_food_stamps.2025_cuts: FY2025 budget reconciliation (P.L. 119-21) reduced federal nutrition spending by $186.5B over 10 years (CBO estimate)
snap_food_stamps.work_requirements_expanded: Adults up to age 64 must meet work requirements (up from age 54)
snap_food_stamps.state_cost_sharing: States responsible for 5% of benefit costs starting October 1, 2027

crop_insurance_lobbying.federal_costs_2022: $17.3B — ~$12B subsidizing premiums, rest for insurance company admin costs
crop_insurance_lobbying.premium_subsidy_rate: Government pays ~61% of premiums on average (crop year 2023)
crop_insurance_lobbying.lobbying_push: Both Democratic and Republican farm bill proposals included language to increase crop insurance for the biggest agricultural operations
crop_insurance_lobbying.beneficiaries: Private approved insurance providers receive admin/operating subsidies from FCIC on top of premium subsidies

tyson.note: Part of $87.5M joint settlement with Cargill; preliminary approval December 17, 2025

cargill.note: Part of $87.5M joint settlement with Tyson

price_fixing_settlements.remaining_defendants: JBS USA and National Beef Packing — litigation ongoing

bayer_monsanto.result: Created world's largest seed and crop chemicals company; controls 25%+ of global seed and pesticides market

dow_dupont.result: Merged then split into Corteva Agriscience (agriculture), Dow (materials), DuPont (specialty)

market_concentration.before: 6 companies controlled 2/3+ of global pesticide and seed markets (2016)
market_concentration.after: 3-4 companies now control ~2/3 of global pesticide and seed markets

syngenta_corteva_lawsuit.allegation: Illegal pay-to-block scheme — loyalty programs requiring distributors to source 85-99% of products from Syngenta/Corteva to block generic competitors
syngenta_corteva_lawsuit.status: Discovery throughout 2025; Trump administration and 12 states seeking October trial date

john_deere_ftc_investigation.background: Deere signed 2023 MOU with American Farm Bureau promising repair access, but provided 'impaired tools and inadequate disclosures'
john_deere_ftc_investigation.epa_ruling_2026: EPA ruled against Deere's attempt to use Clean Air Act to block independent repair

bayer_monsanto.farm_bill_lobbying_goal: Seeking federal preemption to block Roundup/glyphosate lawsuits via Farm Bill language
bayer_monsanto.state_lobbying: Lobbied in Idaho, Iowa, Missouri for pesticide immunity laws in 2024; paid $123,250 in Iowa alone in 2025

corteva_agriscience.note: Spun off from DowDuPont in 2019; defendant in FTC antitrust suit

cargill.20_year_increase: 106% increase in lobbying spending over past two decades

archer_daniels_midland.note: Ingredients found in products from soft drinks to toothpaste

tyson_foods.trend: Doubled annual lobbying spending in past two decades

american_farm_bureau_federation.role: Nation's largest general farm organization; key ally of pesticide/chemical industry in legislative fights

national_pork_producers_council.revolving_door: 12 of 18 lobbyists in 2023 previously held government jobs

croplife_america.role: Trade association for pesticide/crop protection industry
croplife_america.activities: Runs frequent ad campaigns targeting D.C. policymakers; lobbies against state pesticide regulation

total_farm_subsidies.three_year_total_2021_2023: $55.2B across three types of taxpayer-funded farm support programs

trump_trade_war_bailout.justification: Compensate for lost exports from China tariff retaliation
trump_trade_war_bailout.distribution: Top 1% received 13% of payments (~$177K avg); bottom 80% received $5,136 avg; top half collected 95% of total
trump_trade_war_bailout.subsidy_explosion: Farm subsidy payments ballooned from $4B (2017) to $20B+ (2020)

2025_tariff_bailout.amount: $12B in 'one-time bridge payments' to American farmers

distribution_inequality.top_10_percent: Collect ~74% of all commodity subsidy payments (2023)
distribution_inequality.farms_receiving_nothing: 69% of U.S. farms did not collect any subsidy payments
distribution_inequality.repeat_recipients: 10,249 recipients got farm subsidies every year for 39 consecutive years (1985-2023), collecting $11.18B total ($1.09M avg each)
distribution_inequality.top_repeat_recipients: Top 10 individuals collected $9-20M each over 39 years

sugar_program.overview: One of the most politically protected subsidy programs in U.S. agriculture. Zero-cost to taxpayers on paper (loan program), but costs consumers $3.5B+ annually through artificially inflated prices

key_players.fanjul_brothers: Alfonso and Jose 'Pepe' Fanjul — billionaire brothers; own world's largest cane sugar refiner/marketer (Domino, Florida Crystals brands); Pepe is close Trump ally

political_spending_2024.combined_pac_spending: $7.5M+ from American Crystal Sugar, Florida Crystals, American Sugar Cane League, and US Sugar PACs

sugar_program.lobbying_tactics: Sugar industry paid for House members' trips; deployed former House Ag Committee Chair Rep. Collin Peterson as lobbyist
sugar_program.political_evolution: Republican Study Committee called for elimination of sugar protections (2016), softened to 'phasing out' (2019), then endorsed industry-backed alternative plan (2024)

legislative_immunity_efforts.federal: Bayer lobbying for Farm Bill provision to preempt state failure-to-warn lawsuits
legislative_immunity_efforts.state_laws_passed: North Dakota and Georgia signed pesticide immunity laws
legislative_immunity_efforts.trump_executive_action: Invoked Defense Production Act to promote domestic glyphosate production, potentially granting manufacturer immunity
legislative_immunity_efforts.2026_status: Farm bill and Trump's glyphosate order described as 'watershed moment' for pesticides

pfas_forever_chemicals.overview: PFAS exposure linked to cancer, heart damage, hormone disruption, liver/thyroid problems, immune suppression, reproductive issues, and abnormal fetal development

industry_lobbying.regulatory_capture: EPA chemical regulation office headed by two former American Chemistry Council lobbyists and one former American Soybean Association lobbyist under Trump

epa_actions_under_trump.pesticide_approvals: 5 PFAS-based pesticides proposed for registration in less than a year; 2 finalized
epa_actions_under_trump.crops_affected: Tomatoes, peas, lettuce, and other food crops
epa_actions_under_trump.drinking_water: Retained PFOA/PFOS limits but rescinded standards for 4 other PFAS; compliance deadline extended to 2031

pfas_forever_chemicals.lobbying_documents: 500+ pages of FOIA emails, internal presentations, and talking points detail industry efforts to gut federal PFAS regulations

farmworker_protections.regulatory_gap: Farmworkers' toxic chemical exposure does NOT fall under OSHA jurisdiction like virtually all other U.S. workers
farmworker_protections.heat_safety: OSHA proposed heat protection rule in August 2024; several states developing independent rules
farmworker_protections.industry_opposition: CropLife America, American Farm Bureau, and allied agricultural organizations lobby against enhanced pesticide and worker safety regulations
farmworker_protections.farm_bill_2024: Included provisions making it harder for states to regulate pesticides and blocking individuals from seeking compensation for chemical harm

political_spending_overview.2023_combined: $10M+ on political contributions and lobbying (some at all-time highs)

jbs_political_influence.inaugural_donation: $5M to Trump's 2025 Inaugural Committee via subsidiary Pilgrim's Pride (single largest donation — 5x Meta/Amazon contributions)
jbs_political_influence.nyse_listing: JBS began trading on NYSE June 13, 2025 — 6 months after inaugural donation; SEC listing approved within 2 days of Trump appointee Paul Atkins becoming SEC Chair
jbs_political_influence.brazil_bribery: Batista brothers (JBS founders) confessed in 2017 to bribing 1,900 Brazilian politicians; paid $27M U.S. anti-corruption settlement in 2020
jbs_political_influence.lobbying_trend: Doubled lobbying spending; followed by Tyson and Cargill pouring millions into campaign contributions

usda_regulatory_capture.packers_and_stockyards_act: Rep. Andy Harris introduced appropriations bill to nix all enforcement of USDA's new poultry transparency ruling
usda_regulatory_capture.meat_institute_deregulation: Meat Institute president penned letter to Trump White House seeking rollback of Clean Water Act enforcement, salmonella inspections, and equity provisions in Packers and Stockyards Act
usda_regulatory_capture.trump_meatpacker_investigation: DOJ announced investigation into meatpacking companies in late 2025

ag_gag_laws.definition: State laws criminalizing undercover filming or photography of activity on farms without owner consent
ag_gag_laws.states_with_laws: Multiple states including Iowa, Utah, North Carolina, Kansas, Montana, North Dakota, and others

recent_legislation.south_dakota_2025: S.B. 114 — prohibits 'deception to gain access to or employment at an agricultural facility' with intent to cause harm

court_rulings.iowa: Federal appeals court reversed lower courts (Jan 2024) allowing enforcement, then U.S. District Court ruled latest Iowa ag-gag law unconstitutional under First Amendment
court_rulings.north_carolina: Fourth Circuit ruled undercover investigations are protected newsgathering under First Amendment

maha_opposition.overview: Food industry lobbying surged to record levels in 2025 in response to RFK Jr.'s Make America Healthy Again (MAHA) movement targeting ultra-processed foods, synthetic dyes, and food additives
maha_opposition.industry_front_group: Americans for Ingredient Transparency — backed by Coca-Cola, Kraft Heinz, Nestlé; pushing for national labeling preemption laws
maha_opposition.argument: Companies claim MAHA regulations will raise food prices, betting affordability concerns resonate with voters
maha_opposition.state_lawsuits: Major food/beverage lobbying groups have sued states over food safety regulations

soda_tax_opposition.historical_spending: Coca-Cola averaged $6M/year, PepsiCo $3M/year, American Beverage Association $1M/year on lobbying (2011-2015) during soda tax fights
soda_tax_opposition.2018_total: $22.3M in food/beverage lobbying including $5.4M Coca-Cola, $2.8M PepsiCo
soda_tax_opposition.tactics: Industry uses lawsuits and preemption laws to block local soda tax and warning label efforts
soda_tax_opposition.snap_soda_ban: Big Sugar ramped up lobbying as SNAP soda purchase ban proposals spread in states

fda_nutrition_labeling.added_sugar_labels: FDA delayed front-of-package labeling implementation, attributed to industry pressure

school_meals.2024_standards: USDA announced new standards: first-ever added sugar limits in school meals (breakfast cereals ≤6g/dry oz, yogurt ≤2g/oz, flavored milk ≤10g/8 fl oz)
school_meals.industry_opposition: Food companies claimed standards too severe, would cause food waste, spent millions lobbying

renewable_fuel_standard.mandate: ~15B gallons of biofuels (mostly corn ethanol) blended into gasoline annually

lobbying_spending.2012_cycle_pac_donations: $2.2M from corn ethanol interests (large jump from 2008/2010)

iowa_caucus_influence.dynamic: Iowa's first-in-the-nation caucus status gave ethanol outsized influence — presidential candidates routinely visited ethanol factories and pledged RFS support
iowa_caucus_influence.example: 9+ presidential candidates including Trump visited Iowa ethanol factories in 2016
iowa_caucus_influence.declining_influence: Democrats moved first primary to South Carolina; Iowa Renewable Fuels Association acknowledged they 'can't sugarcoat' the concern about declining political leverage
iowa_caucus_influence.ted_cruz_effect: Industry feared Cruz's 2016 Iowa win (despite questioning RFS) would 'open the floodgate' for candidates to oppose ethanol mandates

soybean_exports.china_share_decline: U.S. share of Chinese soybean imports fell from 49% (2012) to 27% (2024)

beef_exports.china_ban_march_2025: China let export licenses for hundreds of U.S. beef facilities expire
beef_exports.impact: Monthly U.S. beef exports to China fell 90%+

china_trade_war_impact.total_ag_trade_with_china: Chinese imports of U.S. agriculture fell from $42.8B (2022) to $29.25B (2024)

first_trump_term.distribution: Top 1% received ~$177K avg; bottom 80% received $5,136 avg; top half collected 95% of total payments

second_trump_term.farmer_sentiment: 48% of farmers surveyed (Dec 2024) said U.S. agriculture 'likely' or 'very likely' at risk of trade war significantly decreasing exports

sources: OpenSecrets (opensecrets.org) — federal lobbying and campaign finance data
Union of Concerned Scientists — 'Cultivating Control' report on farm bill lobbying
Environmental Working Group (EWG) — Farm Subsidy Database and analysis
Agriculture Dive / Food Dive — industry reporting
Federal Trade Commission — Syngenta/Corteva antitrust litigation
Congressional Research Service — Farm Bill primers and SNAP analysis
USDA Economic Research Service — farm bill spending data
U.S. Government Accountability Office — crop insurance reports
The Hill — farm bill lobbying analysis
Civil Eats — PFAS pesticide and food policy reporting
Farm Policy News (University of Illinois) — trade war and lobbying coverage
Sentient Media — meat industry political influence analysis
Mighty Earth — JBS inaugural donation reporting
NPR / PBS — ethanol and Iowa caucus coverage
